headline: Solar Power Dominates EU Grid in Historic June 2026 Milestone
meta_description: Solar energy generated 52 TWh—a quarter of EU electricity in June 2026, surpassing nuclear, gas, and wind combined for the first time.
summary_short: For the first time, solar power generated more electricity than any other source across the EU, with Germany and Spain leading the charge.
body:
A quiet revolution reached a visible milestone across Europe this June: solar panels generated 25 percent of the European Union's electricity—52 terawatt-hours—beating every other energy source.

Nuclear came second at 21 percent, followed by gas at 15 percent, wind at 14 percent, hydro at 12 percent, and coal at just 8 percent. The shift reflects years of investment paying off in real grid data.

Three countries showed what's possible at scale. Germany led with 36 percent of its electricity from solar, Spain close behind at 34 percent, and Poland at 24 percent. These aren't wealthy outliers—Poland's achievement demonstrates the technology works across different geographies and economies.

The June numbers matter because summer sunlight is strongest then, yet the broader trend is clear: renewable energy infrastructure is now reshaping how Europe powers itself, one sunny day at a time.
